# gpui-starter

> A production-ready Rust boilerplate for building desktop applications with GPUI.

## What it is

gpui-starter is a boilerplate project for building desktop apps with [GPUI](https://github.com/zed-industries/zed), the GPU-accelerated UI framework by the Zed team. It includes multi-page navigation, 21 built-in themes, internationalization, form validation, a command launcher, desktop notifications, secure credential storage, and undo/redo. Everything you need to skip the boilerplate and start building.

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hmziqrs/gpui-boilerplate.git gpui-app
cd gpui-app
cargo run
```

Requires Rust (2024 edition). macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools. Linux: Vulkan + X11/Wayland headers. Windows: in progress.

## Features

- **Multi-page architecture** — sidebar navigation with 6 views (Home, Form, Settings, About, Diagnostics, Notifications)
- **21 themes** — JSON theme files loaded via gpui-component. Catppuccin, Dracula, Nord, Gruvbox, Tokyo Night, Solarized, One Dark, Rose Pine, Everforest, Kanagawa, and more. Live switching via Cmd+K.
- **Internationalization** — English and Chinese (zh-CN) via es-fluent / Mozilla Fluent
- **Form validation** — derive macros via gpui-form + koruma validation rules
- **Command launcher** — Cmd+K fuzzy search across all app actions
- **Desktop notifications** — native OS notifications with automatic backend selection and in-app toast fallback
- **Secure storage** — OS keychain integration via keyring crate (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service)
- **Undo/redo** — command-pattern undo/redo stack with keyboard shortcuts
- **macOS system tray** — icon with quick-access menu
- **Single instance** — prevents multiple app instances with IPC forwarding
- **SQLite** — local data persistence via rusqlite in WAL mode
- **Diagnostics** — built-in diagnostics page showing app state, logs, and system info

## Tech stack

- Rust (2024 edition)
- GPUI (GPU-accelerated UI framework from Zed)
- gpui-component (UI component library)
- gpui-form (form builder with derive macros)
- es-fluent (Mozilla Fluent i18n)
- koruma (validation)
- rusqlite (SQLite)
- keyring (OS credential store)
- single-instance (single app instance)
- undo (undo/redo stack)
- accesskit (accessibility)

## Project structure

```
src/
├── main.rs             # Entry point
├── app.rs              # AppRoot — main window
├── root.rs             # Root layout component
├── views/              # Page views (home, form, settings, about, diagnostics, notifications)
├── i18n/               # Fluent translation files (en.ftl, zh-CN.ftl)
├── launcher.rs         # Cmd+K command launcher
├── sidebar.rs          # Sidebar navigation
├── routes.rs           # Page routing
├── undo_stack.rs       # Undo/redo stack
└── ...                 # Storage, tray, menus, shortcuts, notifications, etc.
themes/                 # 21 JSON theme files (not Rust code)
```

## Documentation

Docs at https://gpui-starter.hmziq.xyz/docs/

- **Getting Started** — install, run, prerequisites
- **Architecture** — GPUI patterns, entities, globals, context
- **Forms** — validated forms with gpui-form and koruma
- **Themes** — 21 built-in themes, hot-reload, runtime switching
- **Internationalization** — es-fluent setup, translation files
- **Performance** — preventing render loops, idle CPU
- **Command Launcher** — Cmd+K fuzzy search overlay
- **Notifications** — native OS notifications with fallback
- **Secure Storage** — OS keychain via keyring
- **Routing & Navigation** — sidebar navigation, enum-based routing
- **Testing** — unit tests and integration harnesses

- Testing GPUI applications
